research_method: er002_topic_adapter.make_topic_research_fn (OpenAI Responses API, model gpt-5.6-luna, web_search tool)

candidates.description: The United Kingdom announced a default midnight-to-6 a.m. social-media curfew for 16- and 17-year-olds, although teenagers will be able to override the setting.
candidates.primary_source_note: UK Department for Science, Innovation and Technology's official government announcement (GOV.UK).
candidates.two_point_split_note: 1) The practical question: will a default overnight limit improve teenagers' sleep and concentration? 2) The policy question: does a rule users can easily override offer meaningful protection, or mainly a symbolic tool?

candidates.description: New U.S. government data showed consumer prices fell 0.4 percent from May to June 2026, the largest one-month decline since April 2020, although prices were still 3.5 percent higher than a year earlier.
candidates.primary_source_note: Official U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics Consumer Price Index release.
candidates.two_point_split_note: 1) The everyday-life angle: which household costs became cheaper? 2) The bigger-picture angle: why can prices fall for one month while people still feel life is expensive?

candidates.description: A randomized Oxford-led study found that eight weeks of salsa classes were associated with lower depression and social-anxiety symptoms and greater daily happiness among young adults.
candidates.primary_source_note: University of Oxford's official research announcement and peer-reviewed publication in Psychological Medicine.
candidates.two_point_split_note: 1) The personal-life angle: why might structured social activity like dancing help people who find ordinary socializing difficult? 2) The evidence angle: how strong is the research, and what are its limits?
